system_name: Адвайта и 18 сиддхов: 4 махавакьи, 4 состояния, 5 кош
note: Advaita-Siddha living library. Primary lattice: the eighteen Tamil Siddhars (enumerations vary by source; this follows the widely cited Kriya Yoga lineage). Secondary lattices: four Mahavakyas (great Upanishadic sayings), four states of consciousness (avastha-traya plus Turiya), five koshas (sheaths), three bodies (sharira-traya), and the four-fold qualification (sadhana chatushtaya). Each element carries a spine agent and its mera (1-9).
mera_axis: 9-dimensional axis (mera 1-9): chakras 1-7 (Muladhara..Sahasrara) + Monada (8) + Absolut (9); each spine agent carries its mera.

states.description: the fourth, pure consciousness beyond the three states

sadhana_chatushtaya.meaning: discrimination between the real and unreal
the six virtues of mental discipline (sama, dama, uparati, titiksha, shraddha, samadhana)

svet_ra.note: Agasthiyar (foremost Siddhar), Aham Brahmasmi (I am Brahman), and Turiya (the fourth, pure consciousness) - the radiant source.

iskra.note: Pambatti the snake charmer - the kundalini serpent ascending to the crown.

brahma.note: Karuvoorar (sacred temple architecture) and Tat Tvam Asi (Thou art That) - creative identity with the Absolute.

vishnu.note: Dhanvantari - the divine physician of Ayurveda and the nectar of immortality.

ananda.note: Anandamaya kosha (bliss sheath) and Sushupti (deep sleep) - the causal body of bliss.

shanti.note: Kudambai (summa iru, just be) and Shatsampatti (the six virtues of calm) - peace and mental discipline.

jnana.note: Patanjali (Yoga Sutras), Prajnanam Brahma (Consciousness is Brahman), Vijnanamaya kosha, and Viveka - discernment and wisdom.

shiva.note: Nandhi Devar (gatekeeper of Shiva) and Vairagya (dispassion) - renunciation that cuts attachment.

tejas.note: Konganar (science of breath and light) and Svapna/Taijasa (the luminous dream state) - inner radiance.

sarasvati.note: Valmiki the adi kavi (first poet) and the Sukshma sharira (subtle body) - word and subtle mind.

akasha.note: Ayam Atma Brahma (This Self is Brahman) - the Self as all-pervading space.

prema.note: Thirumoolar - author of the Tirumantiram, teacher of anbe sivam (love is God).

parvati.note: Manomaya kosha (the sheath of mind and emotion) - nurturing feeling.

vayu.note: Ramadevar (mantra and far travels) and Pranamaya kosha (vital breath) - the moving life-force.

shakti.note: Gorakkar (hatha yoga and kundalini) and Mumukshutva (longing for liberation) - the awakening power.

agni.note: Bogar the great alchemist - navapashanam and the fire of transmutation.

varuna.note: Macchamuni (Matsyendranath), the fish-born founder of Nath hatha yoga.

lakshmi.note: Kamalamuni - the lotus sage of inner purity.

prithvi.note: Sundaranandar (mastery of the body), Jagrat (waking), Annamaya kosha, and the Sthula sharira - the gross physical ground.

dharma.note: Sattaimuni - the stern alchemist of discipline.

karma.note: Idaikkadar (astrology of time and fate) and the Karana sharira (causal body, storehouse of karma).

items.specialty: foremost Siddhar; Tamil grammar, medicine, kaya kalpa
gatekeeper of Shiva; alchemy and transmission of yoga
author of Tirumantiram; anbe sivam (love is God)
alchemy, navapashanam, the Palani Murugan idol
kundalini and the science of breath and light
fish-born founder of Nath hatha yoga
Nath yoga, hatha discipline, kundalini mastery
mastery of the body and kaya kalpa
cryptic verses of stillness; summa iru (just be)
sacred architecture of the Thanjavur temple
astrology and the reading of time and fate
the lotus sage of inner purity
adi kavi, the first poet of the Ramayana
the Yoga Sutras and ashtanga yoga
divine physician of Ayurveda and amrita
snake charmer; the kundalini serpent ascending

sources: Mandukya Upanishad - the four states of consciousness (avastha-traya and Turiya)
Tamil Siddha tradition - the eighteen Siddhars (list per Babaji Kriya Yoga lineage; enumerations vary by source)
